meta.title: Nágila Decastro
meta.description: Wife, mother of two girls, homeschool educator, and consultant to mothers. Sleep consulting, first steps to literacy, and parenting with direction.

quote.text: The home cared for first holds everything else up.

body: I am Nágila, wife to Ewerton, mother to Helena and Catharina, living on Cape Cod. Every birth rewrote what I understood about rest, presence and home.
I learned firsthand that a baby's sleep is not a technical detail, it is the oxygen of an entire family. When a mother sleeps again, she returns to herself.

I have homeschooled my two daughters from day one. Not as a rejection of the world, but as a deep yes to time, the time that does not come twice, the time it takes to pass on character at the child's own pace.

Today I walk alongside mothers and families who want more than to survive motherhood and parenting, grounded in serious training, not guesswork. Families who want to live inside it all with clarity, ease and purpose.
Helena arrived in 2021. Catharina in 2024. Ewerton in 2017.
Before any mother I walk alongside, there is this home. It is the home I speak from, the one I return to, the place where everything I teach was first lived.

services.sectionSubtitle: Three paths, one root: caring for the mother and the home. Tap a consultation to see how it works and start a conversation.

cards.description: A human, personalized plan for your baby's sleep, honoring the bond, the child's temperament and your family's real rhythm. No harsh formulas, no guilt.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nágila, I came from your site, I'd like to know more about Sleep Consulting

detail.intro: If you're here, sleep is not happening. Maybe it never has. Maybe it did for a while and then regressed. Maybe you can't even remember the last full night you slept, and you're exhausted enough that you don't know what to try anymore.
whyBody: A baby's sleep is not a technical detail. It is the oxygen of the family. Sleep-deprived mothers lose clarity, patience and self-trust, and the relationship with the partner, the other children, and their own faith silently pays the price.
It is not weakness, it is physiology. And there is a real path forward, grounded in infant sleep neuroscience and respect for the bond, with no rigid method and no promise of the impossible.
journeyBody: I listen first. I don't arrive with a ready-made plan for your baby, I arrive with questions to understand the bond, your child's temperament, and the realistic rhythm of your home. Individual assessment before any technique: every baby is a baby.
I work as a Specialized Sleep Consultant with serious training, no letting the baby cry it out, no one-size-fits-all protocol. Sleep and behavior are not separate problems; I treat them together.
Then we leave with an honest plan: what you do tonight, what we adjust next week, what we revisit together. Ongoing support throughout the process, not a one-time consult.
forWhomItems: Mothers of babies and young children from 0 to 3 years
Mothers tired of method X, method Y, formulas that ignore who your child actually is
Mothers who do not want to use cry-it-out and are looking for a respectful path
Mothers who want to sleep again without giving up who they are
detail.ctaWhatsappText: Hi Nágila, I came from the Sleep Consulting page, I'd like to talk about my baby's sleep

cards.description: How to guide your child through the first steps of reading and writing, from pre-reading to early literacy, with method and without rushing.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nágila, I came from your site, I'd like to know more about the First Steps consultation (pre-reading and literacy)

detail.intro: Your child is old enough to start reading and writing, and you want to be part of it, not hand it off. But the doubts pile up: which letter first? Sounds or syllables? What if I teach it wrong and hold them back? You don't need an expensive workbook or a teaching degree. You need a clear path, at the child's pace, that starts with early literacy and can grow with your family.
whyBody: The first steps into reading mark a child for life. Rushed and pressured, they build a block and the belief that learning hurts. Done with method and presence, they build a child who enjoys learning, and that is worth more than any grade.

I taught both of my daughters to read at home and went on to educate them fully. I know where children usually get stuck, what to do when it feels like there's no progress, and how to keep it light without giving up the method.
And the best part: literacy is only the beginning. Once you understand how your child learns, the same path opens the door to educating at home for real, if and when you choose to take that step.
journeyBody: We start from the ground up: how a child learns to read and write, what comes before the first letter (the pre-reading stage), which method fits your child and your routine, and how to turn 15 minutes a day into real progress, without tears or fights.

Then I show you the natural next step: how these first steps extend into a full home education, if that is what you want. Legalities where you live, choosing a method, building a routine, integrating more than one child, the father's role, and what to do in the weeks you think you'll quit.
This is ongoing consulting, not a one-off class. You have someone in the first week, the tenth, and the hundredth, when the excitement fades and the method has to hold the house together.
forWhomItems: Mothers of children in the pre-reading and literacy stage (around ages 3 to 6)
Anyone who wants to teach their child to read at home and is afraid of doing it wrong
Families testing literacy at home before deciding on homeschooling
Mothers already considering home education who want to start with the most natural path
Families with more than one child balancing different ages
Christian families wanting to align faith and learning at home
detail.ctaWhatsappText: Hi Nágila, I came from the First Steps page, I'd like to talk about literacy and home education

cards.description: Parenting with clear direction: authority with affection, a routine that holds the home together, and character formed day by day, in place of improvisation and endless negotiation.
cards.whatsappText: Hi Nágila, I came from your site, I'd like to know more about the Parenting with Direction consultation

detail.intro: Raising children stopped being leading and became reacting: we put out fires, negotiate all day and end up exhausted, unsure we're going anywhere. As a Certified Parental Educator and a mother who educates her own daughters, I help you give direction back to parenting, choosing each gesture with intention.
whyBody: Most families today parent by improvisation: reacting to behavior, copying what they saw at home, outsourcing to school or screens. The result is a generation of tired parents and anxious children, with no direction.

A home with direction has authority instead of endless negotiation, order and predictability that give the child emotional security, firm limits set with respect, and character formed day by day, not rewarded for good behavior.
Direction is not rigidity. It is knowing where the family is going and leading with firmness and affection, one gesture at a time.
journeyBody: We start from your real family: each child's temperament, the rhythm of your marriage, what is already working and what is draining you. Not manual theory; every home enters differently.

We work on your everyday life: how to restore authority without turning to authoritarianism, how to build routines (meals, prayer, sleep, rest) that hold the home on their own, and how to form character without outsourcing it to school, screens or chance.
Regular meetings, continuous adjustments, real-life support, not loose theory in a workbook.
forWhomItems: Parents who feel raising kids became a 24-hour negotiation
Families tired of reacting, who want to raise with direction
Couples who want to align how they parent before their kids grow up taking sides
Families of faith who want their parenting to reflect what they believe, lived at home every day
Parents who want intentionality in raising their kids, without rigidity or improvisation

products.sectionSubtitle: Practical materials so you don't have to wait for the next conversation to begin.

items.description: Free PDF with the basics for you to start organizing your baby's sleep today, even before any consultation.
20 illustrated cards on emotions and self-control. Children learn to notice, name and respond to what they feel, calmly and without yelling. Digital file to print at home.
40 double-sided cards of grammar, phonology and punctuation to teach reading through play. Digital file to print at home.
25-page e-book on how to design a home routine that holds everything else up: sleep, education, marriage, faith. Read in one evening, apply this week.

contact.titleLine1: If something here spoke to you,
contact.subtitle: Real motherhood, intentional self-care, and the life of a home that chose time. No performance, no pre-packaged recipe.
